title: Trump’s $1.8 Billion Fund Tests Constitutional Limits
description: The deal the president reached with his own subordinates relies on a mechanism created by Congress that legal experts had warned was subject to manipulation.
author: Adam Liptak

title: Ebola Outbreak in Central Africa Could Last Months, W.H.O. Says
description: Health officials reported more than 130 suspected deaths and 540 cases in the Democratic Republic of Congo and Uganda, but experts say the figures could be much higher.
author: Lynsey Chutel, Yan Zhuang and Ephrat Livni

title: Trump’s Slush Fund Will Reward Criminals. Americans Will Pay for It.
description: The president’s corruption and subversion of democratic tradition risk becoming the norm.
author: The Editorial Board

title: A Cattle Ranch Is Doing What Ivy League Colleges Can’t
description: College education does almost nothing to develop the sense that what we do in our day-to-day lives resonates with people beyond ourselves.
author: Michal Leibowitz

title: Barney Frank, Gay Pioneer and Liberal Stalwart in Congress, Dies at 86
description: Often voted the “brainiest,” “funniest” and “most eloquent” member of the House, he was also the first to come out voluntarily and helped normalize being openly gay in public office.
author: Katharine Q. Seelye

title: Now Give Me Money (That’s What I Want)
description: Trump has molded the presidency in his image. It isn’t pretty.
author: Jamelle Bouie

title: Early War Goal Was to Install Hard-Line Former President as Iran’s Leader
description: An Israeli strike designed to free Mahmoud Ahmadinejad from house arrest in Tehran, U.S. officials said, was part of an effort to bring about regime change and put him in power.
author: Mark Mazzetti, Julian E. Barnes, Farnaz Fassihi and Ronen Bergman

title: Prison to Pardons to Payouts: Jan. 6 Rioters Are Elated at Trump’s $1.8 Billion Fund
description: The possibility that people who ransacked the Capitol could get money from the government they attacked is the latest head-spinning twist in President Trump’s effort to rewrite the history of Jan. 6.
author: Alan Feuer

title: With Trump’s Deal, a Possible $100 Million I.R.S. Penalty Melts Away
description: The tax service argued that the Trump Organization tried to claim the same losses twice. The president said the audit was a “disgrace.”
author: Russ Buettner

title: Hatred of Israel and the Degradation of the West
description: Feeding narratives to the credulous isn’t speaking truth to power.
author: Bret Stephens

title: Where Are the Republicans Who Put America First?
description: The “Trump First” wing of the G.O.P. is in ascendance. The rest of the party needs to fight back.
author: Thomas L. Friedman

title: I.R.S. to Drop Audits of Trump and Family
description: As part of the Justice Department’s deal, officials vowed not to pursue any matters, including those involving President Trump’s tax returns, that are pending.
author: Alan Feuer, Andrew Duehren and Glenn Thrush

title: Woman Dies After Falling Into Open Manhole in Manhattan
description: The woman plunged 10 feet, and a passer-by heard her screams. Con Edison said video footage suggested the manhole cover had been dislodged by a truck 12 minutes earlier.
author: Ashley Southall

title: Senate Votes to Take Up Measure to Force Trump to End Iran War
description: With four Republican backers, Democrats won a vote to advance a resolution that would force the president to end hostilities or win authorization from Congress.
author: Megan Mineiro

title: Trump’s Special Envoy to Greenland Receives a Cold Welcome From Locals
description: After President Trump’s threats to seize the island, Gov. Jeff Landry’s offers of MAGA hats and chocolate chip cookies fall flat.
author: Maya Tekeli and Jeffrey Gettleman

title: Trump Endorses Ken Paxton in Texas Senate Runoff Race Against John Cornyn
description: The president embraced Ken Paxton, a MAGA loyalist, over Senator John Cornyn, despite warnings from Republican leaders about Mr. Paxton’s history of scandal.
author: Shane Goldmacher

title: Republican Senators Are Livid at Trump’s Endorsement of Paxton
description: News that President Trump had snubbed Senator John Cornyn of Texas, the four-term incumbent, was met with shock, anger and fear that the G.O.P. could lose his seat.
author: Michael Gold, Megan Mineiro and Carl Hulse

title: ‘The Future of Truth’ Contains Quotes Made Up by A.I.
description: Steven Rosenbaum, author of “The Future of Truth,” said he had started his own investigation after The New York Times asked about the fake quotes.
author: Benjamin Mullin

title: The IRS Thought it Could Fight Trump’s Lawsuit, but it Struck a Deal Anyway
description: Officials wrote a memo outlining ways to challenge President Trump’s suit against the Internal Revenue Service. The administration is instead creating a compensation fund.
author: Andrew Duehren

title: Trump’s Approval Sinks Amid Unpopular War, Darkening G.O.P. Prospects
description: With the midterms nearing, President Trump’s approval rating has hit a second-term low as voters question his handling of the economy, according to the latest New York Times/Siena poll.
author: Lisa Lerer, Ruth Igielnik and Camille Baker
